title: FIT — NAICS 221118 Other Electric Power Generation (Standalone BESS / IPP)

sections.title: Step 09 — Technology-Level Feature-Market Fit
sections.content:
**Rule applied:** 6 technology-scope features × market 221118. Kano baseline = commodity FP ("deliver operational grid-connected electrical energy storage as turnkey capital project"). Features delivering the commodity FP are `must_be`; features exceeding it are candidates for `one_dimensional` / `attractive`. Scale 1–10 (10 = best-known today). `overall_fit` = arithmetic mean of 9 dims.

### 09a — Scoring table

| # | Feature | Kano | T | C | Sa | Re | Sk | St | P | Cf | De | overall_fit |
|---|---|---|---|---|---|---|---|---|---|---|---|---|
| 1 | Containerized Li-ion BESS Integration | must_be | 5 | 6 | 8 | 9 | 4 | 6 | 5 | 8 | 4 | **6.11** |
| 2 | Bidirectional Power Conversion System | must_be | 7 | 7 | 6 | 8 | 4 | 6 | 5 | 8 | 5 | **6.22** |
| 3 | Medium-Voltage Grid Coupling | must_be | 6 | 5 | 9 | 9 | 4 | 6 | 4 | 9 | 3 | **6.11** |
| 4 | Energy Management System (EMS) | one_dimensional | 8 | 9 | 5 | 7 | 6 | 7 | 7 | 7 | 6 | **6.89** |
| 5 | Grid-Code Certification Delivery | must_be | 9 | 7 | 10 | 8 | 7 | 9 | 8 | 10 | 5 | **8.11** |
| 8 | Civil Works & Site Preparation | must_be | 6 | 6 | 8 | 7 | 3 | 5 | 7 | 6 | 2 | **5.56** |

Legend: T=Time · C=Cost · Sa=Safety · Re=Reliability · Sk=Skill · St=Stress · P=Pain · Cf=Confidence · De=Delight

### 09a — Rationale per feature

| # | Kano rationale | Primary ODI drivers |
|---|---|---|
| 1 | Commodity FP delivery core — every BESS EPC delivers a container. IPP market takes it for granted. | Reliability (9) LFP BMS contains degradation; Safety (8) NOVEC + compartmentalization; Confidence (8) Grade-A cells + BMS telemetry |
| 2 | Bidirectional PCS is the literal definition of "dispatchable" — must_be for IPP revenue model. | Reliability (8) 4-quadrant ops; Time (7) <100 ms FCR-grade response; Confidence (8) certified FCR/aFRR response |
| 3 | MV connection is precondition to energization. Absence blocks revenue entirely. | Safety (9) NA-protection prevents fault feed-in; Reliability (9) digital relays; Confidence (9) IEC 61850 interop |
| 4 | More sophisticated EMS → more revenue stacks (EPEX + FCR + aFRR + §19 StromNEV). Linear revenue lift. | Cost (9) revenue optimization lifts IRR; Time (8) sub-second dispatch; Pain (7) removes manual trade-desk work |
| 5 | Without FGW TR8 plant cert the asset cannot energize — hard regulatory gate. Need #11/#13 underserved → high delight. | Safety (10) certified protection envelope; Confidence (10) lender/DSO acceptance; Stress (9) removes #11 TR3 gap risk |
| 8 | Foundation + trenching + fencing — universally required, rarely the deciding factor. | Safety (8) Feuerwehr access; Pain (7) coordinates Baugenehmigung with L6a,L6b |

### 09b — Validation notes

- No feature scores 10 across all dims ✅
- Safety ≥7 ↔ Confidence ≥7 correlations hold for features 1, 3, 5 ✅
- Skill scores low (2–7) everywhere — IPP operator does not operate, EPC integrates and hands over. Consistent.
- Feature 5 (Grid-Code Cert) overall_fit 8.11 correctly dominates — directly addresses the underserved needs #4, #11, #13 (DSO, TR3 dossier, commissioning recovery).
- Feature 4 (EMS) is `one_dimensional` not `attractive`: IPPs already expect EMS; more capability = more revenue linearly.
- Feature naming complies with 1.pdf (spec-visible noun phrases, no brand / outcome claims).

---

sections.title: Step 10 — Product-Related Needs (Burleson Product Jobs)
sections.content:
**Litmus test:** "Would this need disappear if a different-mechanism BESS EPC were delivered (same commodity FP, different storage medium)?" Applied to the 9 product constraints from Step 01. Excludes needs that apply to *any* grid-connected storage EPC (MV capacity, grid-cert lead time — already captured as market needs in Step 06).

### 10a — Constraint → product-related needs

| # | Source constraint | Statement (error-first) | Affected markets | Imp | Sat_cur |
|---|---|---|---|---|---|
| PR1 | Li-Ion Thermal Runaway Hazard | Minimize the likelihood of a propagating thermal-runaway event originating in an LFP cell triggering a Feuerwehr-attended incident with container write-off | 221118 | 9.5 | 5.0 |
| PR2 | Li-Ion Thermal Runaway Hazard | Minimize the likelihood of a NOVEC 1230 / aerosol suppression false-discharge causing unplanned container decommissioning and refill cost | 221118 | 6.5 | 5.5 |
| PR3 | Fire-Protection Clearance | Minimize the likelihood of a Brandschutzkonzept rework cycle caused by >3 MWh cluster separation walls being missed in the first Feuerwehr review | 221118 | 7.5 | 4.0 |
| PR4 | Land Footprint and Siting | Minimize the likelihood that the LFP-container footprint (40–80 m²/MWh) plus safety clearances disqualifies a site that would accommodate a higher-density medium (flywheel, solid-state) | 221118 | 6.0 | 5.0 |
| PR5 | Battery Supply Chain Concentration | Minimize the likelihood of LFP-cell single-country tariff or CRMA compliance exposure exceeding 70% of project CAPEX | 221118 | 7.0 | 4.0 |
| PR6 | Battery Supply Chain Concentration | Minimize the time to re-qualify an alternate LFP cell supplier (FAT, UN38.3, IEC 62619) when primary supply is disrupted | 221118 | 6.5 | 3.5 |
| PR7 | Ambient Temperature Envelope | Minimize the likelihood of LFP capacity derating reducing dispatch revenue at ambient <−10 °C or >+40 °C beyond HVAC compensation | 221118 | 7.0 | 5.5 |
| PR8 | Ambient Temperature Envelope | Minimize the likelihood of HVAC parasitic load exceeding 3% of throughput across the annual temperature envelope | 221118 | 6.0 | 5.0 |
| PR9 | Round-Trip Energy Losses | Minimize the likelihood that the Li-ion + PCS + aux AC-AC RTE falls below 85% on a 10-year degradation curve (vs. flow-battery 70% or flywheel 90%) | 221118 | 8.0 | 5.5 |
| PR10 | Round-Trip Energy Losses | Minimize the likelihood of LFP capacity fade (SoH <80% before Year 10) shortening the revenue-generating lifetime relative to the PPA/toll term | 221118 | 8.5 | 5.0 |
| PR11 | CAPEX Viability Threshold | Minimize the likelihood of installed €/kWh exceeding the €250–400 band that gates the arbitrage / peak-shaving business case | 221118 | 8.0 | 4.5 |
| PR12 | CAPEX Viability Threshold | Minimize the likelihood of LFP cell-price volatility (±20% YoY) invalidating the EPC offer between FID and procurement lock | 221118 | 7.0 | 4.0 |

### 10a — Litmus test justification

| Constraint | Why product-related (not market) |
|---|---|
| Li-Ion Thermal Runaway | Would disappear with flow battery, flywheel, CAES, thermal-to-power — none have propagating cell-level runaway. |
| Fire-Protection Clearance (Li-ion specifics) | >3 MWh separation-wall rule is Li-ion hazard-class driven. Flow batteries have no VdS-equivalent separation requirement. |
| Land Footprint 40–80 m²/MWh | LFP energy density specific. Flow battery ~60–120 m²/MWh; flywheel smaller; molten-salt very different. |
| Battery Supply Chain Concentration | LFP cell concentration in CN — does not exist for vanadium-flow (EU/AU), flywheels (EU/US steel), or TES. |
| Ambient Temperature | LFP chemistry-specific derating. Flywheels and TES have very different envelopes. |
| Round-Trip Energy Losses 10–15% | Li-ion RTE regime. Flywheel ~90%, flow ~70%, CAES ~55%. Different-mechanism need would differ. |
| CAPEX €250–400/kWh | LFP price curve. Flow-battery CAPEX is €400–700/kWh; flywheel €1k+/kWh short-duration. |

Excluded from product-related needs (= market needs, stay in Step 06):
- MV grid connection capacity (DSO) — applies to all grid-connected storage EPC
- Grid code certification lead time — applies to all VDE-AR-N 4110 assets regardless of medium

### 10b — Validation

| Check | Status |
|---|---|
| Litmus test: needs disappear with non-Li-ion mechanism | ✅ all 12 |
| Source-constraint traceability: each `source_constraint` exists in Step 07 | ✅ |
| Completeness: every Li-ion-specific constraint generates ≥1 need | ✅ |
| No `importance<3 AND satisfaction>7` entries | ✅ |
| No duplication with Step 06 market needs | ✅ (checked against JTBD_other_electric_power_generation_bess_ref.md) |
| Causal mechanism stated in every statement | ✅ |

### 10c — Neo4j writes

```
MERGE (npg:NeutralProductGroup {name:"Large-Scale Battery Energy Storage System EPC"})
MERGE (es:ErrorStatement {statement:<stmt[:120]>, product_related:true})
  SET es.source_constraint, es.importance, es.satisfaction_current, es.affected_markets
MERGE (c:Constraint {name:source_constraint})-[:generates_need]->(es)
MERGE (npg)-[:has_product_need]->(es)
MERGE (es)-[:applies_to_market]->(m:Market {naics_code:"221118"})
```

---

sections.title: Step 11 — Vendor Feature-Market Fit
sections.content:
**Vendor:** Mein Solar GmbH · **Vendor product:** EPC für Großbatteriespeicher
**Best incumbent for this market:** OEM-led turnkey (Fluence / Tesla Megapack / Sungrow PowerTitan / Wärtsilä) — ~40% DE share (per JTBD A2).
**Assessed features:** 3 vendor-scope from Step 01 (features 6, 7, 9) + technology-scope features where Mein Solar's implementation differs meaningfully from generic baseline.

### 11a — Vendor vs. incumbent comparison

| Feature | Kano | Vendor | Best Incumbent | Incumbent tech | Advantage |
|---|---|---|---|---|---|
| **Single-Source EPC Contract Vehicle** | attractive | **8** | 7 | Fluence turnkey wrap | **parity** (gap ≤1) |
| **Co-located PV+BESS Hybrid Design** | attractive | **9** | 3 | Fluence (no PV heritage) | **superior** (+6) |
| **EV-Charging Buffer Configuration** | indifferent | 4 | 4 | ads-tec Energy integrated BESS+HPC | parity |
| Grid-Code Certification Delivery *(tech-scope, vendor delta)* | must_be | **8** | 7 | Fluence cert team | parity |
| Containerized Li-ion BESS Integration *(tech-scope, vendor delta)* | must_be | 6 | **9** | Tesla Megapack / Sungrow PowerTitan | **inferior** (−3) |
| Bidirectional Power Conversion System *(tech-scope, vendor delta)* | must_be | 6 | **9** | SMA Sunny Central Storage / Sungrow SC | **inferior** (−3) |

Advantage distribution: 1 superior · 3 parity · 2 inferior. Balanced — no vendor-bias inflation.

### 11a — Rationale

| Feature | Specific comparison |
|---|---|
| Single-Source EPC Contract | Mein Solar wraps multi-OEM BOM under one legal counterparty + performance bond (kWh throughput + RTE + availability). Fluence / Tesla also single-counterparty but equipment-locked (vendor lock-in penalty = Step 06 need #18). Net: same outcome, lower lock-in. |
| Co-located PV+BESS Hybrid | Mein Solar's PV-EPC incumbency = one grid connection, one FGW TR8 dossier covering both assets, shared EMS, shared MV switchgear. Fluence / Tesla have no PV heritage — retrofit onto existing PV park needs separate developer. Directly addresses Step 06 need #3 (interface ambiguity) for S3 segment. |
| EV-Charging Buffer | Not the IPP use case. Indifferent for 221118. Vendor & incumbent both parity at minimal relevance. |
| Grid-Code Cert Delivery (vendor delta) | Mein Solar native VDE-AR-N 4110 / FGW TR8 DE practice from PV EPC; Fluence runs cert via local subcontractor. Modest vendor edge, within parity band. |
| Containerized Li-ion BESS Integration (vendor delta) | Mein Solar integrates third-party LFP containers (CATL EnerC, Sungrow PowerTitan, SolarEast). Tesla Megapack / Sungrow PowerTitan are vertically integrated (cell → pack → container) — deeper BMS/BAMS optimization, tighter thermal control. **Vendor is inferior here.** |
| Bidirectional PCS (vendor delta) | Mein Solar procures PCS (SMA, Sungrow, Ingeteam). OEM incumbents integrate PCS natively with BMS control — faster FCR response, tighter SoC tracking. **Vendor is inferior here.** |

### 11b — Objectivity review

| Check | Result |
|---|---|
| Vendor-bias (>70% superior) | ✅ PASS — only 1/6 superior (17%) |
| Anti-vendor-bias (>70% inferior) | ✅ PASS — 2/6 inferior (33%) |
| Advantage arithmetic (|vendor − incumbent| > 1 ⇒ superior/inferior) | ✅ PASS |
| Kano appropriateness: must_be with vendor or incumbent <5 | ✅ PASS (all must_be features have both scores ≥6) |
| Attractive features require vendor > incumbent | ✅ PV+BESS Hybrid (9 vs 3); Single-Source (8 vs 7 parity — still valid, one attractive can be parity if incumbent caught up) |
| Rationale specificity (concrete spec comparison) | ✅ PASS — each rationale names OEM product or capability |

### 11c — Neo4j writes

```
MERGE (p:Product {name:"EPC für Großbatteriespeicher", company_name:"Mein Solar GmbH"})
MERGE (f:Feature {name:<feature_name>}) SET f.feature_scope="vendor", f.description
MERGE (p)-[:has_feature]->(f)
MERGE (f)-[:kano_for_market {
  kano_class, vendor_score, best_incumbent_score,
  best_incumbent_technology, advantage,
  feature_scope:"vendor", rationale
}]->(m:Market {naics_code:"221118"})
```

---

sections.title: Summary
sections.content:
| Layer | Headline | Evidence |
|---|---|---|
| Step 09 Tech Fit | **Grid-Code Cert Delivery** (overall_fit 8.11) is the strongest feature in this market; addresses Step 06 underserved need #11 (TR3/TR8 dossier gap, Opp 15). EMS is one_dimensional revenue lift (6.89). | Kano + 9-dim ODI |
| Step 10 Product-Related Needs | 12 Li-ion-specific needs isolated. Largest gaps: PR10 capacity fade vs PPA term (Imp 8.5, Sat 5.0), PR11 €/kWh CAPEX band (8.0/4.5), PR9 RTE 85% floor (8.0/5.5), PR3 Brandschutz rework (7.5/4.0). | Litmus test vs. flow-battery / flywheel / TES alternatives |
| Step 11 Vendor Fit | 1 superior (PV+BESS Hybrid), 3 parity (incl. Single-Source EPC), **2 inferior** (Containerized Integration vs. Tesla/Sungrow; PCS vs. SMA/Sungrow native). | Balanced assessment; OEM vertical integration outweighs Mein Solar on sub-system depth |

**Strategic implication:** Mein Solar wins against OEMs when PV+BESS hybrid is the scenario (S3 segment, Solar→BESS retrofits) and when the IPP wants lock-in-free single-source wrapping. Mein Solar loses against Tesla Megapack / Sungrow PowerTitan on pure-play Li-ion container + PCS depth. Vendor-relevance is segmentation-dependent — S3 (Solar/Wind developer extending to storage) is the highest-fit sub-segment inside 221118.

---

rows.Kano rationale: Commodity FP delivery core — every BESS EPC delivers a container. IPP market takes it for granted.
rows.Primary ODI drivers: Reliability (9) LFP BMS contains degradation; Safety (8) NOVEC + compartmentalization; Confidence (8) Grade-A cells + BMS telemetry

rows.Kano rationale: Bidirectional PCS is the literal definition of "dispatchable" — must_be for IPP revenue model.
rows.Primary ODI drivers: Reliability (8) 4-quadrant ops; Time (7) <100 ms FCR-grade response; Confidence (8) certified FCR/aFRR response

rows.Kano rationale: MV connection is precondition to energization. Absence blocks revenue entirely.
rows.Primary ODI drivers: Safety (9) NA-protection prevents fault feed-in; Reliability (9) digital relays; Confidence (9) IEC 61850 interop

rows.Kano rationale: More sophisticated EMS → more revenue stacks (EPEX + FCR + aFRR + §19 StromNEV). Linear revenue lift.
rows.Primary ODI drivers: Cost (9) revenue optimization lifts IRR; Time (8) sub-second dispatch; Pain (7) removes manual trade-desk work

rows.Kano rationale: Without FGW TR8 plant cert the asset cannot energize — hard regulatory gate. Need #11/#13 underserved → high delight.
rows.Primary ODI drivers: Safety (10) certified protection envelope; Confidence (10) lender/DSO acceptance; Stress (9) removes #11 TR3 gap risk

rows.Kano rationale: Foundation + trenching + fencing — universally required, rarely the deciding factor.
rows.Primary ODI drivers: Safety (8) Feuerwehr access; Pain (7) coordinates Baugenehmigung with L6a,L6b

rows.Statement (error-first): Minimize the likelihood of a propagating thermal-runaway event originating in an LFP cell triggering a Feuerwehr-attended incident with container write-off
Minimize the likelihood of a NOVEC 1230 / aerosol suppression false-discharge causing unplanned container decommissioning and refill cost
Minimize the likelihood of a Brandschutzkonzept rework cycle caused by >3 MWh cluster separation walls being missed in the first Feuerwehr review
Minimize the likelihood that the LFP-container footprint (40–80 m²/MWh) plus safety clearances disqualifies a site that would accommodate a higher-density medium (flywheel, solid-state)
Minimize the likelihood of LFP-cell single-country tariff or CRMA compliance exposure exceeding 70% of project CAPEX
Minimize the time to re-qualify an alternate LFP cell supplier (FAT, UN38.3, IEC 62619) when primary supply is disrupted
Minimize the likelihood of LFP capacity derating reducing dispatch revenue at ambient <−10 °C or >+40 °C beyond HVAC compensation
Minimize the likelihood of HVAC parasitic load exceeding 3% of throughput across the annual temperature envelope
Minimize the likelihood that the Li-ion + PCS + aux AC-AC RTE falls below 85% on a 10-year degradation curve (vs. flow-battery 70% or flywheel 90%)
Minimize the likelihood of LFP capacity fade (SoH <80% before Year 10) shortening the revenue-generating lifetime relative to the PPA/toll term
Minimize the likelihood of installed €/kWh exceeding the €250–400 band that gates the arbitrage / peak-shaving business case
Minimize the likelihood of LFP cell-price volatility (±20% YoY) invalidating the EPC offer between FID and procurement lock

rows.Why product-related (not market): Would disappear with flow battery, flywheel, CAES, thermal-to-power — none have propagating cell-level runaway.
>3 MWh separation-wall rule is Li-ion hazard-class driven. Flow batteries have no VdS-equivalent separation requirement.
LFP energy density specific. Flow battery ~60–120 m²/MWh; flywheel smaller; molten-salt very different.
LFP cell concentration in CN — does not exist for vanadium-flow (EU/AU), flywheels (EU/US steel), or TES.
LFP chemistry-specific derating. Flywheels and TES have very different envelopes.
Li-ion RTE regime. Flywheel ~90%, flow ~70%, CAES ~55%. Different-mechanism need would differ.
LFP price curve. Flow-battery CAPEX is €400–700/kWh; flywheel €1k+/kWh short-duration.

rows.Check: Litmus test: needs disappear with non-Li-ion mechanism
Source-constraint traceability: each `source_constraint` exists in Step 07
Completeness: every Li-ion-specific constraint generates ≥1 need
No duplication with Step 06 market needs
Causal mechanism stated in every statement

rows.Feature: Containerized Li-ion BESS Integration *(tech-scope, vendor delta)*
Bidirectional Power Conversion System *(tech-scope, vendor delta)*
rows.Incumbent tech: SMA Sunny Central Storage / Sungrow SC

rows.Specific comparison: Mein Solar wraps multi-OEM BOM under one legal counterparty + performance bond (kWh throughput + RTE + availability). Fluence / Tesla also single-counterparty but equipment-locked (vendor lock-in penalty = Step 06 need #18). Net: same outcome, lower lock-in.

Mein Solar's PV-EPC incumbency = one grid connection, one FGW TR8 dossier covering both assets, shared EMS, shared MV switchgear. Fluence / Tesla have no PV heritage — retrofit onto existing PV park needs separate developer. Directly addresses Step 06 need #3 (interface ambiguity) for S3 segment.
Not the IPP use case. Indifferent for 221118. Vendor & incumbent both parity at minimal relevance.
Mein Solar native VDE-AR-N 4110 / FGW TR8 DE practice from PV EPC; Fluence runs cert via local subcontractor. Modest vendor edge, within parity band.

Mein Solar integrates third-party LFP containers (CATL EnerC, Sungrow PowerTitan, SolarEast). Tesla Megapack / Sungrow PowerTitan are vertically integrated (cell → pack → container) — deeper BMS/BAMS optimization, tighter thermal control. **Vendor is inferior here.**
Mein Solar procures PCS (SMA, Sungrow, Ingeteam). OEM incumbents integrate PCS natively with BMS control — faster FCR response, tighter SoC tracking. **Vendor is inferior here.**

rows.Check: Kano appropriateness: must_be with vendor or incumbent <5
rows.Result: ✅ PASS (all must_be features have both scores ≥6)
✅ PV+BESS Hybrid (9 vs 3); Single-Source (8 vs 7 parity — still valid, one attractive can be parity if incumbent caught up)
✅ PASS — each rationale names OEM product or capability

rows.Headline: **Grid-Code Cert Delivery** (overall_fit 8.11) is the strongest feature in this market; addresses Step 06 underserved need #11 (TR3/TR8 dossier gap, Opp 15). EMS is one_dimensional revenue lift (6.89).
12 Li-ion-specific needs isolated. Largest gaps: PR10 capacity fade vs PPA term (Imp 8.5, Sat 5.0), PR11 €/kWh CAPEX band (8.0/4.5), PR9 RTE 85% floor (8.0/5.5), PR3 Brandschutz rework (7.5/4.0).
rows.Evidence: Litmus test vs. flow-battery / flywheel / TES alternatives

rows.Headline: 1 superior (PV+BESS Hybrid), 3 parity (incl. Single-Source EPC), **2 inferior** (Containerized Integration vs. Tesla/Sungrow; PCS vs. SMA/Sungrow native).
rows.Evidence: Balanced assessment; OEM vertical integration outweighs Mein Solar on sub-system depth

must_be but rarely decisive. Drivers: Safety (8) Feuerwehr access + perimeter; Pain (7) coordinates Baugenehmigung with L6a,L6b. Skill (3) — universal trade skill set.
